Off Calendar Sep-23-2016 Continued To Nov-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jeff Fackler of Michel & Fackler (925-933-1309) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/cross-complainent/opposing party, Transworld Constructio Inc.

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

CROSS-DEFENDANT CITY and COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Transworld has pled present and existing actual controversies as to contract interpretation that may be the subject of declaratory relief. (CCP 1061.) Also, Transworld asserts third-party beneficiary theories not susceptible to demurrer. As to equitable indemnity, "an act that constitutes a breach of contract may also be tortious;" whether that is so here must await further litigation. (See Acadia, California, Ltd. V. Herbert (1960) 54 Cal.2d 328, 336.) Whether CCSF is immune under the facts here also must be litigated. (See Gov't Code 815(a), 815.2(a).) Court grants 30 days leave to answer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
